items.title: OpenAI Newsroom | Product | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet:
OpenAI has been active with major announcements heading into April 2026. Most notably, the company closed a record $122 billion funding round on April 2, 2026, which it plans to use to accelerate the next phase of AI development — focusing on deep integration into science, industry, and everyday work. On the product side, Codex now offers pay-as-you-go pricing for teams, and ChatGPT received updated integrations with Box, Notion, Linear, and Dropbox, adding new write-capable actions.

On the mod

items.title: ChatGPT in 2026: Pricing, Models and Features That Actually Matter
items.author: www.gend.co
items.snippet: OpenAI has been actively releasing new models and features in early 2026. The most notable recent release is GPT-5.3-Codex, described as their most capable agentic coding model, combining Codex and GPT-5 training stacks for best-in-class code generation, reasoning, and general-purpose intelligence — approximately 25% faster and setting new benchmark highs. Additionally, GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano were released to the Chat Completions and Responses API, targeting high-volume workloads with fas

items.title: ChatGPT Release Notes - April 2026 Latest Updates - Releasebot
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet: OpenAI has released several significant API updates in early 2026. On the model side, GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano were introduced to the Chat Completions and Responses API, bringing high-capability models optimized for speed and cost efficiency in high-volume workloads. The inference stack for existing models (GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex) was also optimized, running approximately 40% faster for API customers. Additionally, the Sora video API was expanded with reusable character references, longe

items.title: OpenAI Retires GPT-4o and Daily AI Updates for April 2026 - AI and News
items.author: www.aiandnews.com
items.snippet:
OpenAI has been active on Twitter/X in early April 2026 with several major announcements. Most notably, the company announced it raised $122 billion to accelerate the next phase of AI development, and announced Codex now offers pay-as-you-go pricing for teams. OpenAI also officially retired the GPT-4o model from all plans effective April 3, 2026, while rolling out GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro to ChatGPT users with advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows.

items.title: Advancing science and math with GPT-5.2
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has published several notable research papers and initiatives in early 2026. Key safety-focused work includes CoT-Control (March 5, 2026), studying how reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought and reinforcing monitorability as an AI safety mechanism, and IH-Challenge (March 10, 2026), which trains models to prioritize trusted instructions to improve resistance to prompt injection attacks.
